description_long: This house features a sprawling, single-story layout with a complex, multi-sectioned red-tiled hip roof. The exterior walls are finished in a muted reddish-brown color, and the structure sits on a striped base that suggests a stylized yard or patio area. Large rectangular windows and door openings are distributed asymmetrically across the facade, providing ample natural light and access. The roof overhangs create shaded areas around the perimeter, and a smaller attached section on one side appears to function as an auxiliary room or garage. The overall design is practical and robust, with a focus on horizontal lines and a low profile. The house exudes a Mediterranean or Southwestern architectural influence, characterized by its earthy tones and tiled roofing.
description: A single-story house with a red-tiled hip roof, reddish-brown walls, and large, asymmetrically placed windows and doors. The design is low-profile and practical, with Mediterranean or Southwestern influences.
description_view_0: Front view shows the main entrance area with two large rectangular openings and the prominent red-tiled roof.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the extended section of the house, additional windows, and the attached auxiliary room or garage.
description_view_2: Rear view highlights the back of the auxiliary section and the layered roof structure, with minimal window openings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays more window and door openings, as well as the overhanging roof and striped base.
attribute_entryway_design: Main entryway features a large rectangular door opening, no visible steps, and a modest overhang from the tiled roof.
